summary: Counts trailing zeros of a u64 via a shift-and-count while loop (`y >>= 1; count += 1` while `y & 1 == 0`); canonical two-stage Hoare-triple-to-equation proof (`Spec.MonoLoopCombinator.while_loop` → `RustM.Triple_iff_BitVec`) with a multiplicative loop invariant `x₀.toNat = y.toNat * 2 ^ count.toNat ∧ 0 < y.toNat ∧ count.toNat < 64` linking accumulator and witness through exponentiation, termination by the bit-level measure `tzTerm = y.toNat` halved each iteration — the body step lemma bridges `y &&& 1 = 0 ⇒ y.toNat % 2 = 0` via `Nat.and_one_is_mod` to derive `y ≥ 2`, discharges `count + 1 < 2^32` no-overflow from `c < 64`, and re-derives the `c+1 < 64` invariant clause by bounding `2^(c+1) ≤ x₀ < 2^64`. Three contract clauses (range `r < 64` / divisibility `2^r ∣ x` / exactness `(x >>> r) &&& 1 = 1`) are projected from a single master existential `trailing_zeros_u64_nonzero_spec`, with a totality theorem casing on `RustM.toBVRustM` for the no-panic clause and a powers-of-two diagonal theorem closing `r = k` by combining divisibility (`Nat.pow_dvd_pow_iff_le_right` gives `r ≤ k`) with exactness (`2^(k-r) % 2 = 1 ⇒ k - r = 0`).
